seoTitle: How to Find a Missing Folder in Outlook Easily (2026)
metaDescription: Track down a lost or hidden Outlook folder by searching for a known email, checking the Mailbox Size tool, and expanding collapsed folder trees.
intro: A client folder can easily get dragged into the wrong place or buried inside a collapsed subfolder tree without you noticing. Instead of expanding every folder one by one, Outlook has built-in tools that can locate exactly where a missing folder ended up, using either search or the account's folder size properties list.

steps.heading: Search for a known email inside the missing folder
steps.body: Type a unique keyword you know exists in one of the folder's messages into Outlook's search bar to locate it.

steps.heading: Check the email's properties
steps.body: Open the found email and view its properties to see the exact root folder location it currently lives in.

steps.heading: Use the Mailbox Size utility
steps.body: Right-click your account's root folder, choose Properties, then open Folder Size to see a complete text list of every folder and subfolder in your mailbox.

steps.heading: Scan the list for the folder's real location
steps.body: Review the structural text list to confirm the folder's exact name and where it's nested.

steps.heading: Expand and restore the folder
steps.body: Locate and expand the collapsed parent node containing your folder, then drag it back to a more accessible spot in your sidebar if needed.

faq.question: Why did my folder disappear from the sidebar?
faq.answer: It's usually still there but nested inside a collapsed parent folder, rather than actually deleted, so expanding parent nodes often reveals it.

faq.question: How can I find a folder without knowing its exact name?
faq.answer: Search for a specific keyword or sender you know is inside an email from that folder, then check that email's properties to see the folder path.

faq.question: What is the Mailbox Size tool used for here?
faq.answer: It gives you a full text-based list of every folder in your account, which makes it easier to spot a missing folder's exact location than clicking through the sidebar.

faq.question: Could my missing folder actually be in Deleted Items?
faq.answer: Yes, it's worth checking your deleted items archive in case the folder was accidentally removed rather than just misplaced.
